data.title: China Vanke Jointly Invests in Xiamen Housing Rental Fund
data.description:
China Vanke jointly invested in the establishment of the Wanxin Jinshi (Xiamen) Housing Rental Fund.

The fund, which has a committed capital of 1.6 billion yuan, aims to fully acquire a China Vanke subsidiary holding the Bay Community rental housing project in Xiamen in China's Fujian province.

The fund is a joint investment by China Vanke subsidiaries Wanju Yingtong and Shenzhen Vanke as well as CITIC Jinshi, Xinhua Life and Dajia Life. It will be managed by CITIC Jinshi and is structured as a limited partnership with an initial 10-year term.

Shares of China Vanke closed 2% higher on the Shanghai bourse on Wednesday.

data.title: COMMENT-Hot UK CPI makes BoE March hold certain for sterling
data.description:
The rise in UK CPI to a 10-month high of 3.0% in January means the Bank of England will almost certainly keep interest rates unchanged next month.

The 3.0% print was higher than the 2.8% forecast by the BoE and economists, and a full percentage point higher than the BoE's target level.

Consolation for doves was found in UK services CPI printing at a lower than expected 5.0%, having been forecast at 5.2% by the BoE and a Reuters poll of economists.

The cooler than expected UK services CPI print helps explain why markets still see a BoE rate cut in May as more likely than not (a May cut would maintain the cycle of quarterly BoE cuts, a cycle which started last August).

GBP/USD climbed to a nine-week high of 1.2641 ahead of Wednesday's UK inflation data, with GBP/EUR scaling a 2025 peak by 1.21.

Related comments:

For more click on

data.title: Hang Seng Bank: Cheng to Succeed Lee
MicroStrategy to Offer $2 Billion Convertible Notes Offering
data.description:
MicroStrategy said late Tuesday it is planning to offer $2 billion worth of 0% convertible senior notes due 2030 in a private offering.

The company said it expects to grant initial purchasers the option to purchase up to $300 million worth of additional notes within five business days from the date of issue.

The initial conversion rate will be determined at the pricing of the offering, the company said.

MicroStrategy said it plans to use the net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Sterling Reacts Modestly to U.K. Inflation Data as Markets Look at Details — Market Talk
data.description: Sterling rises but only modestly after Wednesday's higher-than-expected U.K. inflation data. This reaction reflects the finer details of the report, ING analyst Francesco Pesole says in a note. Headline inflation rose to 3.0% year-on-year in January but primarily due to an unexpected surge in food prices and markets are "attaching little weight" to this data, he says. Services inflation was also marginally lower than expected. This "benign trend" in services inflation could persist in the second quarter and supports the case for the Bank of England to cut interest rates once a quarter this year, he says. Sterling rises only marginally versus the dollar and euro after the data, last at $1.2621 and 0.8279 per euro. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Russian rouble little changed vs US dollar as analysts urge caution
data.description:
The Russian rouble was little changed against the U.S. dollar on Wednesday as investors awaited further developments on talks between Moscow and Washington on ending the war in Ukraine.

At 0830 GMT, the rouble was up 0.3% at 91.25 against the dollar on the Moscow Exchange, close to a five-month high reached last week. But some analysts urged caution and said excessive optimism was premature.

"The events are bringing an excessively optimistic mood and volatility to the market," BSC brokerage analysts said. "It too early to talk about real changes."

The rouble was also up 0.2% against China's yuan, the most traded foreign currency in Russia.

The Russian currency has gained about 20% against the dollar this year, mostly on expectations of easing geopolitical tensions between Russia and the West.

data.title: Hong Kong's Hang Seng Bank names new chairman
data.description: Hong Kong-based Hang Seng Bank said on Wednesday that the incumbent Deputy Chairman Edward Cheng will succeed Irene Lee as the chairman of the board.

data.title: Sibanye-Stillwater Enhances Marikana Contract, Signs Chrome Agreements
data.description:
By Maitane Sardon

Sibanye-Stillwater said it made enhancements to its Marikana Contract and signed a new Chrome Management Agreement with the Glencore-Merafe Venture.

The South African mining company said Wednesday that the pact is expected to optimize value from the production of chrome.

The company said it partnered with various third parties, including the Glencore-Merafe Venture, to recover and market chrome produced by its South African operations for platinum group metals. Chrome is an important byproduct of the production of such metals, and its South African operations are a significant producer of global chrome ore.

The enhancements to the Marikana Contract are expected to accelerate the delivery of contracted chrome volumes by approximately 20 years, the company said. This will result in an increase in future cash flow, it said.

Write to Maitane Sardon at maitane.sardon@wsj.com

data.title: Hang Seng Bank: Cheng Will Be Redesignated as Chairman of Board in May >0011.HK
Baidu's Search Market Share Could Be Pressured Amid Competition — Market Talk
data.description: Baidu's search market share could be threatened after Tencent's Weixin began tests to integrate DeepSeek's model, Daiwa analyst John Choi says in a research note. In January, 22% of Baidu's search queries were AI-powered but not monetized, the analyst notes. Although Baidu has followed suit to integrate DeepSeek into its search engine, Choi thinks the high-quality information from companies' and institutions' official accounts on Tencent's Weixin platform could attract more users in the long term, he says. Meanwhile, Baidu's move to make its next-generation AI model Ernie 4.5 open source amid fierce competition could weigh on its operating margin, Choi says. Daiwa lowers Baidu's rating to outperform from buy and cuts its target to HK$100.00 from HK$108.00. Shares last ended 2.05% lower at HK$88.35. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Scandic Capital Markets Day 2025 - A clear path for higher growth
data.description:
Scandic Hotels Group AB

At its Capital Markets Day starting at 13:00 (CET) today, Scandic Hotels Group (“Scandic” or the “Company”), presents an update on its business plan forming the basis of the mid-term targets for 2025 – 2027 as well as the Group’s strategy for 2030.

Jens Mathiesen, Scandic President & CEO, comments:

“In recent years, we have focused on optimization and invested in strengthening our commercial and operational capabilities. We deliver good guest satisfaction, have engaged team members, and we run our business with high efficiency and cost control. In the coming years, our focus will be on growth and continuous improvement to enhance profitability. Through selective portfolio expansion, leveraging our commercial and operational strengths, and a strategic approach to capital allocation, we aim to create an attractive journey for our guests, team members, and shareholders.”

Following the pandemic, Scandic has established a strong operational, commercial, and financial position. In October 2024, the company updated its financial targets and introduced a capital allocation framework for 2024–2026, reflecting its ambition for growth and profitability while maintaining a balanced risk approach in favorable markets. At the Capital Markets Day, Scandic will present the 2030 strategy, focusing on leveraging its portfolio strengths and enhancing its commercial and operational capabilities to drive further value creation.

Strategy for 2030 in brief – A clear path for higher growth

  • Strengthen the #1 position in the Nordics by adding ~7,000 rooms to the lease portfolio.
  • Selective expansion in Germany, by adding ~3,000 rooms to the lease portfolio, focusing on key destinations in the top ten largest cities.
  • Take a leading position in the fast-growing and profitable economy segment through Scandic Go, expected to account for ~50% of signed new rooms.
  • Broaden Nordic presence at low risk with high profitability through 30-40 new franchise hotels.
  • Drive revenues and loyalty through more relevant and personalized guest experiences.
  • Improved operational excellence through investments in new ways of working.

Following today’s announcement of a proposed ordinary dividend of SEK 570 million and the intention to launch a new share buyback program of approximately SEK 500 million. Scandic will distribute a total of approximately SEK 1.9 billion to shareholders through dividends and share buybacks from December 2024 to March 2026.

Scandic will continue to allocate its free cash flow toward attractive investments and dividends while utilizing share buybacks as a recurring tool to optimize shareholder value.

Speakers at the Capital Markets Day will include: Jens Mathiesen, CEO; Isabelle Elmqvist, Head of Strategy and Sustainability; Thérèse Cedercreutz, CCO; Peter Jangbratt, COO Sweden & Denmark; and Pär Christiansen, CFO.

Scandic’s Capital Markets Day start at 13:00 (CET). More information, including link to webcast, can be found on the website here.

 

For more information, please contact:

Rasmus Blomqvist, Director Investor Relations, Scandic Hotels Group

Email: rasmus.blomqvist@scandichotels.com

Phone: +46 702 335 367

Pär Christiansen, CFO, Scandic Hotels Group

Email: par.christiansen@scandichotels.com

Phone: +46 761 802 663

About Scandic Hotels Group

Scandic is the largest hotel company in the Nordic countries with a network of about 280 hotels with 58,000 rooms in operation and under development, in more than 130 destinations. The company is the leader when it comes to integrating sustainability in all operations and its award-winning Design for All concept ensures that Scandic hotels are accessible to everyone. Well loved by guests and employees, the Scandic Friends loyalty program is the largest in the Nordic hotel industry and the company is one of the most attractive employers in the region. Scandic is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm. www.scandichotelsgroup.com. www.scandichotelsgroup.com

data.title: French Stocks Halt 7-Session Winning Streak
data.description:
The CAC 40 edged lower to trade around 8,195 on Wednesday, halting a seven-session streak of gains as investors weighed the impact of escalating trade tensions while absorbing new corporate earnings reports and news.

On Tuesday, US President Donald Trump unveiled the possibility of imposing a 25% tariff on imports of automobiles, semiconductors, and pharmaceuticals, with an announcement anticipated by April 2nd.

On the corporate front, luxury stocks were among the worst performers, with shares of Hermes, LVMH, and Kering dropping 2%, 1.2%, and 1.2%, respectively.

European luxury goods makers have expressed confidence that they could leverage their pricing power to offset any potential tariff impact, with Trump now in office and warning of steep tariffs on European goods.

On a more positive note, STMicroelectronics led the index, soaring 5.4% to reach a three-week high, followed by Thales, which rose around 3.1%, and Societe Generale, up 2.5%.

data.title: Trainline falls to 6-mth low after J.P.Morgan downgrade
data.description:
** Ticketing firm Trainline slips as much as 8.67% to 316p, lowest since August 2024

** Stock second-biggest pct loser on FTSE mid-cap index (.FTMC)

** J.P.Morgan downgrades to "neutral" from "overweight"; cuts PT to 400p from 500p

** Holds cautious view due to increased competition and regulatory uncertainties

** Last month, UK announced plans for new government-backed online train ticket retailer to simplify ticket buying process

** Plan comes ahead of industry-wide consultation on Rail Reform Bill, which aims to establish the Great British Railways (GBR) as the governing body for passenger rail

** JPM says, "Near-term risks from Rail Reform Bill could limit valuation"

** Stock rated "buy" on avg, median PT is 460p - LSEG

** TRNT down ~20% so far this year, as of last close

data.title: DAX Up to New Highs
data.description:
The DAX edged higher toward 22,900 on Wednesday, hitting fresh record highs and extending its winning streak to a third consecutive session.

Investors evaluated new corporate earnings reports and fresh tariff threats while awaiting the latest FOMC minutes for insights into the Federal Reserve's future policy moves.

Meanwhile, attention remained on Ukraine peace negotiations, though meaningful progress is expected to take time.

data.title: Kinder Morgan Completes $640 Million Acquisition of North Dakota Gas System
data.description:
Kinder Morgan said late Tuesday its Hiland Partners Holdings subsidiary completed its $640 million acquisition of a natural gas gathering and processing system in North Dakota from Outrigger Energy II.

The company said the acquired assets include a 270 million cubic-feet per day processing facility and a 104-mile, high-pressure gas gathering pipeline.

Kinder Morgan expects the acquisition to be immediately accretive to shareholders, with a projected 2025 adjusted EBITDA multiple of around 8 times on a full-year basis, it said.

data.title: UK Market Talk Roundup: Shares Losing
data.description:
Stocks on the fall in the UK today. Compiled by Dow Jones Newswires Markets Desk, markets.eu@dowjones.com

0843 GMT - Shares in easyJet and TUI fall, dragged by leisure-travel group peer Jet2's warning that its profit margin could come under pressure. easyJet shares in London trade down 2.9% at 502.60 pence while shares for German travel company TUI are 4% lower at 6.81 euros. Jet2 shares are down 9.65% at 14.14 pounds. Jet2 said that although bookings are up on-year for April, May and June, current macro-economic conditions, pressure on consumer incomes and customers' preference for booking late risk weighing on its performance. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0828 GMT - Glencore switching its listing to the U.S. would be a positive catalyst for its shares, Jefferies analysts Christopher LaFemina and Patricia Hove write. The Anglo-Swiss miner said Wednesday that it is reviewing its primary-listing status. Glencore's $1 billion share buyback also helps ease concerns over the slight miss to 2024 earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization expectations, they write. Meanwhile, Glencore is not expected to benefit from strong commodity tailwinds until next year, they write. Shares trade down 3% at 342.7 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0824 GMT - MTU Aero Engines raised its outlook after delivering earnings which beat expectations, JPMorgan analysts David H. Perry and Lucy Fitzgerald say in a research note. However, the company's cash generation was weaker than expected, which may temper the share price reaction, they say. Fourth-quarter cash generation was 75 million euros below views due to higher-than-expected recall costs on the GTF engine and supply chain challenges, the analysts say. Consensus for cash generation could be trimmed as a result, though earnings consensus for 2025 could rise by a few percentage points, they note. Shares trade 4.7% lower to 330 euros. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

Contact us in London. +44-20-7842-9288
Markettalk.eu@dowjones.com

data.title: UK Market Talk Roundup: Brokers Comments
data.description:
Broker comments in the UK today. Compiled by Dow Jones Newswires Markets Desk, markets.eu@dowjones.com

0843 GMT - Shares in easyJet and TUI fall, dragged by leisure-travel group peer Jet2's warning that its profit margin could come under pressure. easyJet shares in London trade down 2.9% at 502.60 pence while shares for German travel company TUI are 4% lower at 6.81 euros. Jet2 shares are down 9.65% at 14.14 pounds. Jet2 said that although bookings are up on-year for April, May and June, current macro-economic conditions, pressure on consumer incomes and customers' preference for booking late risk weighing on its performance. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0828 GMT - Glencore switching its listing to the U.S. would be a positive catalyst for its shares, Jefferies analysts Christopher LaFemina and Patricia Hove write. The Anglo-Swiss miner said Wednesday that it is reviewing its primary-listing status. Glencore's $1 billion share buyback also helps ease concerns over the slight miss to 2024 earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization expectations, they write. Meanwhile, Glencore is not expected to benefit from strong commodity tailwinds until next year, they write. Shares trade down 3% at 342.7 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0753 GMT - BAE Systems' 2024 results beat market expectations, J.P. Morgan analyst David Perry says. The British aerospace and defense group reported earnings per share 3% ahead of Vuma consensus, while its free cash flow was 890 million pounds above consensus, he says. The company's evolution in 2026 should become clearer in the coming months, once the U.S. administration tables its 2026 defense budget in April, and the U.K. government sets out its military spending plans, Perry notes. "We recommend the shares as a long-term holding; however, after an 11% rally in the last five days we don't expect a major reaction today," he adds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0733 GMT - The FTSE 100 is seen opening little changed, according to IG. The index closed flat to 8766 points on Tuesday. Data Wednesday showed U.K. inflation increased to 3.0% year-on-year in January from 2.5% in December. Economists in a WSJ survey expected 2.8%. Core inflation rose to 3.7% from 3.2% a month ago, as expected. The Bank of England's "gradual and careful" pace of cutting interest rates looks set to continue for the foreseeable future, Pepperstone strategist Michael Brown says in a note. While more rapid rate cuts are possible in the second half amid stalling economic momentum, stubbornly high inflation won't permit this yet. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0711 GMT - HSBC's medium-term guidance for its return on tangible equity will likely be a focus in management's call with analysts and investors, RBC Capital Markets says in a research note after the bank's results. The assumptions underpinning the guidance will prompt questions, analysts Benjamin Toms and Pablo de la Torre Cuevas say. HSBC kept its midterm ROTE guidance unchanged in the mid-teens excluding notable items, while consensus pencils in a 14% return. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0704 GMT - HSBC produced a respectable set of fourth-quarter numbers, Keefe, Bruyette & Woods say in a research note. The bank's adjusted pretax profit beat consensus on better income and costs, which were partially offset by higher impairments, analysts Elise Yu Ge and Edward Firth say. Its $2 billion share buyback feels light against its recent $3 billion run rate but the dividend it proposed was slightly better, they write. HSBC is the most expensive U.K. bank on a price-to-earnings basis, which is difficult to justify for a group delivering little loan growth and targeting mid-teens returns, the analysts say. "Some may argue that HSBC has traditionally traded at a premium to U.K. banks, but to suggest that Asia/Hong Kong is the same opportunity as it was 15 year ago is obviously flawed," they add. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

Contact us in London. +44-20-7842-9288
Markettalk.eu@dowjones.com

data.description:
Hove A/S :

  • HOVE AS GROUP HAS RECEIVED THREE ORDERS IN PAST WEEK WITH TOTAL VALUE OF MORE THAN DKK 3,000,000

data.title: Waystar Holding Launches Secondary Public Offering of 18 Million Shares
data.description:
Waystar Holding said late Tuesday that certain selling stockholders are offering 18 million common shares.

The selling stockholders are being offered by certain investment funds of EQT AB, Canada Pension Plan Investment Board, Bain Capital and their affiliates.

The selling stockholders are planning to grant the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to 2.7 million common shares.

Waystar said it will not receive any proceeds from the offering.

data.title: UK's Jet2 tumbles on rising cost pressures
data.description:
** Jet2 slides 9.3% to 1,420p; biggest loser across London stocks

** British travel company says it continues to experience inflationary input cost pressures exceeding headline CPI rate in hotel accommodations, aircraft maintenances and general airport and Eurocontrol charges

** Expects additional operational costs from aircraft gaps in peak summer on Airbus delivery delays

** Sees 8-10% y/y rise in FY25 profit between 560 mln pounds ($706.9 mln) and 570 mln pounds

** Sale capacity for summer 2025 up 8.5% y/y to 18.6 mln seats

** Jefferies says trading appears robust despite commentary that macro conditions, "demands on consumer discretionary incomes" are driving limited visibility amid continued late bookings

** But, strong trading offset by stronger cost inflation, analysts say

** In 2024, stock rose 26.7%

($1 = 0.7922 pounds)

data.title: Inspire Medical Systems Insider Sold Shares Worth $4,694,766, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Randy Ban, Executive Vice President, Patient Access and Therapy Development, on February 09, 2025, sold 25,584 shares in Inspire Medical Systems for $4,694,766. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Ban has control over a total of 7,592 common shares of the company, with 7,259 shares held directly and 333 controlled indirectly.

data.title: Valmet to Deliver New Automation System to Finnish Hydropower Plant
data.description:
Valmet secured an order from KSS Energia for a new hydro turbine automation system for the Verla 2 hydropower plant in Kouvola, Finland.

The Finnish process technologies and automation company will deliver its Valmet DNA hydro turbine automation system to replace an old third-party system at the plant, according to a Wednesday release. No financial details related to the order were disclosed.

The system will be delivered to the customer in December.
